text: Writing clean code is an essential skill for any programmer. The most important goal is to make your code readable for both yourself and others. You should use clear naming conventions for variables and functions, such as `calculateTotal` instead of `ct`. Functions should perform a single, well-defined task to keep your program organized. By writing clean code, you improve its maintainability, making it easier to find bugs and add new features later. Ultimately, investing time in writing clear code now will save you far more time in the future.
